item.title: Global news you need to know before the U.S. stock market opens on Wednesday
item.description: On Wednesday before the US stock market opened, Hassett criticized the New York Federal Reserve's research on the impact of tariffs. US core durable goods orders in December increased by 0.6% month-on-month, while durable goods orders fell by 1.4%. The New York Federal Reserve's business activity index dropped to -25.7, a five-year low. New housing starts rose to 1.404 million units. Japan's new Prime Minister, Sanae Takaichi, took office, and the European Union supports member countries bordering Russia and Ukraine. UK inflation fell to a 10-month low, and Lagarde may leave office early. US stock futures rose, and the STOXX 600 index reached a record high
item.author: Wallstreetcn

item.title: Billionaire's Prediction: The Market Is Wrong, Fed Will Conduct 'Substantially More' Than 2 Rate Cuts
item.description: Billionaire investor David Einhorn predicts that the Federal Reserve will implement more than two interest rate cuts this year, contrary to market expectations. With Kevin Warsh set to become the new Fed chair, Einhorn believes Warsh will advocate for rate cuts, even in a strong economy, unless inflation surges. Warsh's past hawkish stance raises questions about his approach to balancing rate cuts with the Fed's balance sheet management. Investors should monitor both interest rates and the Fed's balance sheet strategy as they impact market dynamics.
item.author: Motley Fool

item.title: U.S. December Durable Goods Orders Quick Review
item.description: In December, U.S. durable goods orders decreased by 1.4% month-on-month, lower than the expected 2%. Excluding aircraft, non-defense capital durable goods orders increased by 0.6% month-on-month, higher than the expected 0.3%. This increase indicates that capital investment performed robustly at the end of the year amid reduced uncertainty in trade policy. Boeing stated that the number of aircraft orders in December was higher than the previous month, but does not fully correspond to its monthly data
item.author: Wallstreetcn

item.description: In December, new housing starts in the United States increased by 6.2% month-on-month, expected to be 1.1%
item.author: Wallstreetcn

item.title: Industrial metals rise benefiting from the AI industry chain, AI disruption theory drags down US stock performance, Japanese summer fiscal events become the focus ---0218 Macro Dehydration
item.description: Since August 2023, the prices of precious metals and industrial metals have risen significantly, mainly driven by the AI industry chain. Despite the U.S. CPI being lower than expected, concerns about the profitability models of the financial, logistics, and real estate sectors being replaced by AI have led to poor performance in the U.S. stock market. Following the victory of Japan's Liberal Democratic Party, fiscal policy has tightened slightly, and several key fiscal events are expected this summer, including discussions on consumption tax reductions and the announcement of budget requests for the fiscal year 2027
item.author: Wallstreetcn

item.title: Japanese stocks opened higher and continued to rise, with the Nikkei 225 index up over 1%, and spot gold recovered to 4,900 USD
item.description: On Wednesday, Japanese stocks opened higher and continued to rise. By the end of the morning session, the Nikkei 225 index was up 1.2% to 57,253.46 points. The Tokyo Stock Exchange index rose 1.4% to 3,813.05 points. The US dollar rose 0.24% against the Japanese yen, trading at 153.67. Spot gold rebounded above $4,900, with an intraday increase of 0.51%
item.author: Wallstreetcn

item.title: When "aircraft engines" become the target for "data center power supply"
item.description: With the exponential growth of power demand from AI data centers, the lag in expanding traditional power grids has become a common enemy of the industry. Against this backdrop, companies like FTAI Aviation and ProEnergy are accelerating the "dimensional reduction" of aviation power assets into the electricity market
item.author: Wallstreetcn

item.title: Bessenet and Walsh's "mentor," Druckenmiller, "precisely" opened positions in financial stock ETFs, S&P Equal Weight ETFs, and Brazil ETFs in Q4
item.description: In the technology sector, Druckenmiller completely sold off Meta in Q4 and increased his positions in Google and Sea. The "mentor-mentee" relationship between Druckenmiller and Bessenet, Walsh has led the market to speculate that "Druckenmiller economics"—which opposes deficits, inflation, and tariffs—may be infiltrating policy-making through Bessenet and Walsh
item.author: Wallstreetcn

item.title: Wall Street mogul Tepper bets on AI: Micron holdings surge by 200%, new purchase of Korean ETF
item.description: Hedge fund mogul Tepper precisely targeted the AI chip sector in the fourth quarter of last year, investing over $600 million heavily in Micron Technology and the South Korean chip ETF, and has already achieved about 30% returns this year. The global memory shortage combined with the AI computing power frenzy has created a stark contrast between his chip bets and the plummeting software stocks
item.author: Wallstreetcn
